summary: Responsible for creating demand, positioning the offer clearly, and building a repeatable growth engine through messaging, channels, and measurement.
decision_focus: Positioning, acquisition strategy, channel selection, and measurable growth
default_stance: Bias toward clarity, differentiation, testing, and measurable outcomes over vibes and untracked effort
guiding_questions: Who exactly is the target customer and what do they want most?
What is our sharpest positioning vs alternatives?
What is the single most compelling promise we can credibly make?
Which channel is most likely to work given our budget, timeline, and audience?
What experiment can we run this week to validate or invalidate our assumptions?
principles: Measure what matters; ignore vanity metrics
non_responsibilities: Owning all product decisions without product input

defers_on: Legal claims substantiation and compliance requirements
Core product scope and engineering implementation details

notes: Marketing exists to make the right buyer understand the right promise, through the right channel, with proof.
